reasoning: Based on pattern of Trump-Putin engagement in 2025 (Feb and March calls during peace talks) but deteriorating relationship by late April 2025. Trump's frustration with Putin's slow progress and rejection of ceasefire make another call uncertain. While special envoy Witkoff continued meeting Putin, Trump's own patience appeared to be waning with end-of-April deadline missed. Direct leader calls are relatively infrequent even during active negotiations, and public expressions of frustration create political disincentive for another engagement unless significant breakthrough occurs. 38% probability reflects possibility that stalled negotiations may require leader-level intervention, but also recognizes pattern of reduced engagement after failed ceasefire talks.
